challenges.prompt: The fixture test_fixtures/db_in_mount_dashboard.ex has DB queries running twice (once per HTTP mount, once per WS mount) plus an unguarded PubSub subscribe with an insufficiently scoped topic. Produce lib/my_app_web/live/dashboard_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.DashboardLive): (1) move ALL queries (invoices, metrics, unread_count) out of mount into handle_params, (2) gate PubSub.subscribe with if connected?(socket), (3) scope the topic to the user via "dashboard:user:#{current_user.id}", (4) keep the {:new_notification, _} handle_info clause, (5) mount only assigns current_user + empty defaults. All four changes are required.

The fixture test_fixtures/pre_1_7_user_list.ex has both 1.6 template idioms AND queries in mount. Produce lib/my_app_web/live/user_list_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.UserListLive): (1) convert ALL route helpers to ~p, (2) convert live_patch/live_link/live_redirect to <.link>, (3) convert ~L to ~H with :for/:if, (4) move Accounts.list_users out of mount into handle_params, (5) replace push_redirect with push_navigate, (6) convert the full :for loop to use :for attribute.

Write two files: (1) lib/my_app/checkout/form.ex (module MyApp.Checkout.Form) as an Ecto.Schema with `use Ecto.Schema`, `import Ecto.Changeset`, an embedded_schema declaring fields :cardholder, :card_last4, :zipcode, and a changeset/2 that casts + validates them. (2) lib/my_app_web/live/checkout_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.CheckoutLive) that uses MyApp.Checkout.Form (NOT the DB schema MyApp.Checkout.Order directly) for the <.form>. mount builds @form via to_form(Form.changeset(%Form{}, %{})). Decoupling the UI form from the DB schema is the key pattern.

Write lib/my_app_web/live/tenant_orders_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.TenantOrdersLive) that subscribes to "orders:org:#{org_id}" (NOT "orders") gated by connected?/1. The handle_event("create") call broadcasts on the same scoped topic. Additionally, handle_info({:new_order, order}, socket) must pattern-match against order.org_id == socket.assigns.current_org.id as a defensive check, so that even if a broadcast leaks, the UI ignores it. mount assigns :current_org from session. stream(:orders, []).

Write lib/my_app_web/live/settings_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.SettingsLive). The handle_event("validate") must use a single clause with a tiny discriminating head `handle_event("validate", %{"settings" => params}, socket)` — no field destructuring in the head, no binding of assigns in the head. Field-level logic lives in the body via `Enum.reduce` over params or similar. Also write a second clause `handle_event("save", %{"settings" => params}, socket)` for submission. The fixture test_fixtures/function_head_soup.ex shows the anti-pattern.

Write lib/my_app_web/live/projects_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.ProjectsLive) that fixes FOUR anti-patterns simultaneously: (1) DB query in mount → move to handle_params, (2) unguarded PubSub subscribe → add connected?/1, (3) large collection via assign → convert to stream, (4) unscoped PubSub topic → scope to "projects:org:#{org_id}". mount takes %{"org_id" => org_id} from session. All four fixes are required.

Write lib/my_app_web/components/table.ex (module MyAppWeb.Components.Table). Declare table/1 with attr :rows (list, required), attr :row_id (any, default nil), attr :row_click (any, default nil), and slot :col (required, takes attr :label). Additionally, slot :action (takes attr :label). Render <table> with <thead> showing @col.label and <tbody> iterating @rows, each row rendering each :col slot and a trailing :action column. The <tr> must include phx-click binding if @row_click is set.

Write lib/my_app_web/live/invoices_table_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.InvoicesTableLive). Combine filter (?status=), sort (?sort=col&dir=asc), and cursor pagination (?cursor=) via URL params read in handle_params. stream(:invoices, rows, reset: true). Each column header is a <.link patch={~p"/invoices?status=#{@status}&sort=#{col}&dir=#{next_dir}"}>. Pagination links use <.link patch=> as well. mount only sets up empty stream.

Write lib/my_app_web/live/inbox_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.InboxLive). Security requirements: (1) on_mount :ensure_authenticated, (2) scope all queries through %MyApp.Scope{user: current_user, org_id: org_id}, (3) PubSub topic is "inbox:org:#{org_id}:user:#{user_id}", (4) handle_info({:new_message, msg}, socket) double-checks msg.org_id == socket.assigns.scope.org_id before stream_inserting (defense in depth). Multi-layer isolation.

Write lib/my_app_web/components/filter_panel_component.ex (module MyAppWeb.FilterPanelComponent) as a Phoenix.LiveComponent. Critical: it must NOT keep a local :filter copy that drifts from the parent. update/2 always writes the incoming assigns, replacing any previous state. The component's own events are scoped to @myself (phx-target={@myself}), and on change it sends the new value to the parent via send(self(), {:filter_change, new_filter}) — the parent is the source of truth, which will re-invoke update/2 with the new assigns.

The fixture test_fixtures/navigation_mix_up.ex has multiple deprecated navigation idioms. Produce lib/my_app_web/live/orders_live.ex that fixes all three families: (1) push_redirect → push_navigate, (2) live_patch/live_redirect/live_link → <.link patch/navigate=>, (3) ~L → ~H with :for/:if. Additionally, the handle_params/3 block must properly branch on the filter param (with a default _params clause). Must not leave any deprecated call.

Write lib/my_app_web/live/clearable_list_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.ClearableListLive). It must: (1) initialize stream(:items, initial) in mount, (2) handle_event("clear", _, socket) calls stream(socket, :items, [], reset: true) — the reset: true flag is required to clear all DOM children, (3) handle_event("add", %{"name" => name}, socket) stream_inserts a new item. Render <div id="items" phx-update="stream"> with :for iteration. The reset: true on clear is the key point.

The fixture test_fixtures/pre_1_7_user_list.ex is a Phoenix 1.6 LiveView. Produce lib/my_app_web/live/user_list_live.ex that migrates it to Phoenix 1.7+: every route becomes ~p, every <%= live_patch/live_redirect/live_link %> becomes <.link patch/navigate=>, every <%= for %>/<%= if %> becomes :for/:if, ~L becomes ~H, push_redirect becomes push_navigate, and the LiveView loads its users in handle_params/3 (not mount/3). Preserve module name MyAppWeb.UserListLive.

The fixture test_fixtures/assigns_messages_inbox.ex loads ~12000 messages into assigns. Rewrite it as lib/my_app_web/live/inbox_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.InboxLive) using stream/3. The LiveView must: (1) stream(:messages, [], reset: false) in mount, (2) reset/populate the stream in handle_params via stream(..., reset: true), (3) render a container with id="messages" and phx-update="stream", iterating with {dom_id, msg}, (4) handle stream_delete for "delete" events, (5) handle stream_insert at: 0 on {:new_message, m}, (6) track a separate :message_count assign because you cannot Enum.count @streams.

Write lib/my_app_web/components/modal.ex (module MyAppWeb.Components.Modal). Define modal/1 with attrs :id (string, required), :show (boolean, default false), :on_cancel (any, default nil). Declare slot :title, slot :actions, and slot :inner_block (required). Render

{render_slot(@title)}

{render_slot(@inner_block)}
{render_slot(@actions)}
.

The fixture test_fixtures/db_in_mount_dashboard.ex queries the DB in mount/3 and subscribes to PubSub without a connected?/1 guard. Produce lib/my_app_web/live/dashboard_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.DashboardLive). mount/3 must only set up the current_user + initial empty assigns. Subscribing must be gated by if connected?(socket). The queries (invoices, metrics, unread_count) must run in handle_params/3. Keep the handle_info/2 clause.

Write lib/my_app_web/live/signup_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.SignupLive). mount assigns @form = to_form(MyApp.Accounts.change_user(%MyApp.Accounts.User{})). Render a form that displays per-field error messages only if used_input?(@form[:field]) is true. Use <.input field={@form[:email]} /> etc. handle_event("validate", params, socket) re-runs the changeset with action :validate and calls to_form. Must use used_input?.

The fixture test_fixtures/external_poll_price.ex has every connected user polling an external API once per second. Replace this pattern. Produce two files: (1) lib/my_app/price_ticker.ex (module MyApp.PriceTicker) — a GenServer that polls HTTPClient.fetch_price every 1000ms and broadcasts via Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast(MyApp.PubSub, "prices:BTC", {:price, price}); (2) lib/my_app_web/live/price_ticker_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.PriceTickerLive) — subscribes to "prices:BTC" via connected?/1 and handles {:price, price} in handle_info. No :timer.send_interval in the LiveView.

The fixture test_fixtures/navigation_mix_up.ex has a mix of push_redirect, push_patch (ok), live_patch, live_redirect, and live_link. Produce lib/my_app_web/live/orders_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.OrdersLive) using only 1.7 idioms: <.link patch={~p...}> for filter nav, <.link navigate={~p...}> for archived and new, push_navigate for new-order handler, push_patch for filter handler. Convert ~L to ~H and the <%= for %> to :for.

Write lib/my_app_web/components/filter_bar.ex (module MyAppWeb.FilterBar) as a Phoenix.LiveComponent (not a function component). It owns its own :filter assign, has a handle_event("change_filter", %{"value" => value}, socket) that updates its own state AND uses send/2 to notify the parent with {__MODULE__, {:filter_changed, value}}. It must call use MyAppWeb, :live_component. Render <form phx-change="change_filter" phx-target={@myself}>.

Write lib/my_app_web/live/users_table_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.UsersTableLive) that paginates a user table by cursor. mount/3 stream(:users, []). handle_params/3 reads %{"cursor" => cursor} (default nil), loads MyApp.Accounts.list_users(cursor: cursor), and streams with reset: true. "next" button calls push_patch(to: ~p"/users?cursor=#{next_cursor}"). Must stay in the same LiveView — do not use push_navigate.

Write lib/my_app/posts.ex (module MyApp.Posts) with a list_posts/1 that takes a %MyApp.Scope{user: user} struct and returns `from(p in Post, where: p.user_id == ^user.id) |> Repo.all()`. Also define MyApp.Scope as a struct with :user. The function must pin the user id with ^ in the query. No raw Post |> Repo.all() anywhere. Additionally, write lib/my_app_web/live/posts_live_scope.ex (module MyAppWeb.PostsLiveScope) that builds %Scope{user: current_user} and calls Posts.list_posts(scope) in handle_params.

Write lib/my_app_web/live/search_form_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.SearchFormLive) that has a search form with phx-change="search" and a text input with phx-debounce="300". Also handle the case where the user hasn't submitted anything yet — only show results when used_input?(@form[:q]) is true. mount/3 builds @form via to_form(%{"q" => ""}).

Write lib/my_app_web/live/notifications_list_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.NotificationsListLive). It must fix TWO anti-patterns: (1) DB query in mount — move to handle_params; (2) unscoped PubSub topic — use "notifications:user:#{user_id}" instead of "notifications". mount/3 takes %{"user_id" => user_id}, subscribes via connected? guard. Everything else is normal.

frontmatter.description: Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView — component-forward architecture with verified routes, HEEx, streams, forms (nested cast_assoc), async mount, pubsub, on_mount authz, push_navigate. Triggers on liveview, phoenix, heex, stream, form, pubsub. NOT for Phoenix 1.6 or older.
frontmatter.name: elixir-phoenix-liveview-composite

best_skill.skill_md_content:
--- name: elixir-phoenix-liveview-composite description: Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView — component-forward architecture with verified routes, HEEx, streams, forms (nested cast_assoc), async mount, pubsub, on_mount authz, push_navigate. Triggers on liveview, phoenix, heex, stream, form, pubsub. NOT for Phoenix 1.6 or older. --- # Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView — Composite Skill ## Quick Start The parent `Phoenix.LiveView` is a thin coordinator. Anything stateful lives in a `Phoenix.LiveComponent`. The parent owns URL state, session, and PubSub subscriptions; children own their own slice of state and talk upward via `send/2` or sideways via `send_update/2`. All rendering uses HEEx with the `~p` verified-route sigil, and all collections over ~100 items flow through `stream/3`. ## When to use - Writing a new Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView with more than one interactive region - Refactoring a bloated LiveView whose `handle_event` clauses exceed ~10 cases - Reviewing PRs where a single LiveView mutates many unrelated assigns - Converting a Phoenix 1.6 LiveView to 1.7+ idioms (verified routes, `<.link>`, streams, `assign_async`) - Introducing real-time updates, nested forms, or per-resource authorization to an existing LiveView module ## Architectural Skeleton (Foundation) Every LiveView begins as a thin parent. The parent's `mount/3` and `handle_params/3` assign only URL and session state — no feature assigns, no DB queries that render blocking payloads. For each distinct interactive region (a form, a list, a filter panel, a modal) create a `Phoenix.LiveComponent` under `lib/my_app_web/live/components/`. Give every LiveComponent a stable required `:id` assign and render it as `<.live_component module={...} id="..." ... />`. User events are handled inside the component's own `handle_event/3` and mutate only that component's socket. Child-to-parent signaling uses `send(self(), {:child_msg, payload})`, handled by the parent's `handle_info/2`; parent-to-child updates use `send_update/2` targeting a stable `id`. All database work is routed through context modules, called from the component that owns the data — never from the parent. Forms are always built with `to_form/2` inside the component that owns them, never lifted to the parent. The parent never reaches into a child's socket with `assign/3`. ## HEEx + Verified Routes Use the `~p` sigil for every route. `~p"/users/#{id}"` replaces `Routes.user_path(@socket, :show, id)`. Use HEEx directives `:if={...}` and `:for={x <- @xs}` instead of the legacy `<%= if %>` and `<%= for %>` Eex blocks. Prefer `<.link navigate={~p"/users"}>` over the removed `live_link` / `live_redirect` helpers, and `<.link patch={~p"/users?sort=name"}>` for same-LiveView URL updates. Template bodies always use the `~H` sigil. Common mistakes: 1. Mixing old `Routes.*_path` helpers with new `~p` sigils in the same module. 2. Leaving `<%= for item <- @items do %>` blocks that should be `:for={item <- @items}` directives. 3. Using `` where `<.link navigate={~p"/x"}>` is required for client-side routing. ## Function Components + Slots Function components are rendering primitives, not logic holders. Declare every input with `attr :name, :type, required: true` — no untyped assigns. Declare every child region with `slot :inner_block` or a named `slot :actions`. The body is HEEx + `render_slot/1` + `~H` interpolation only. No `case`, no branching on business rules, no Ecto calls, no `Repo.get/2`. If a rendering decision is needed — a badge color for a status, a class for an alert variant — compute it in the parent LiveView via `assign/3` and pass the resolved value in as an attr. Co-locate these components under `MyAppWeb.CoreComponents` or a feature-scoped `*Components` module. Unit-test them with `Phoenix.LiveViewTest.render_component/2` passing literal attrs. Common mistakes: 1. Calling `Repo.get/2` or context functions inside a function component. 2. Using `case @status do ... end` for class selection — precompute in the parent. 3. Accepting a raw `%Ecto.Changeset{}` and branching on `changeset.valid?`. 4. Adding `@socket` or `assign/3` calls — function components have no socket. 5. Omitting `attr` declarations "just for this one prop". ## LiveComponents (Stateful) Treat `update/2` as the single choke point where parent-supplied props become socket assigns. It is the ONLY function that calls `assign(socket, :prop, assigns.prop)` for external data. Any dependent local state (filtered lists, derived labels) is also computed inside `update/2`, in the same pass. `handle_event/3` mutates only component-owned UI state like `:open?` or `:selected_id`; it must never re-read props from `assigns`. When the parent needs to push a mid-lifecycle change, it calls `send_update(MyComponent, id: "x", prop: new_value)`. For child-to-parent signaling, use `send(self(), {:child_event, @myself, payload})` and route it through the parent's `handle_info/2`. The parent's `<.live_component />` call MUST include `:id`. Common mistakes: 1. Calling `assign(socket, :prop, assigns.prop)` inside `handle_event/3`. Props belong in `update/2` only. 2. Forgetting `:id` on `<.live_component />`. Stateful components REQUIRE a stable id. 3. Deriving filtered or sorted data in the template instead of in `update/2`. 4. Using `send_update/3` with partial assigns and expecting old props to persist. 5. Trying to mutate parent state by reaching "up" through assigns. ## Forms + Nested Associations Nested forms use `cast_assoc` plus `<.inputs_for>`. In the schema, declare `has_many :children, Child, on_replace: :delete`. In the changeset, call `cast_assoc(:children, with: &Child.changeset/2, sort_param: :children_sort, drop_param: :children_drop)`. In the LiveComponent that owns the parent form, assign the form once with `to_form(Parents.change_parent(parent))`. Render children with `<.inputs_for :let={cf} field={@form[:children]}>`. Add a child with a button posting `parent[children_sort][]=new`; remove a child with `parent[children_drop][]=` plus a hidden input inside the row. On the `"validate"` event, rebuild via `to_form(changeset, action: :validate)`. On `"save"`, call the context's `update_parent/2`; on `{:error, changeset}`, re-assign `to_form(changeset)`. Common mistakes: 1. Forgetting `on_replace: :delete` on the `has_many` — removed rows reappear after save. 2. Using `<.inputs_for>` without a matching hidden `sort_param` input — row order scrambles. 3. Rebuilding child rows manually in `handle_event` instead of letting `cast_assoc` + `:drop_param` do it. 4. Putting the nested form in the parent LiveView instead of the owning LiveComponent. 5. Calling `to_form/2` on the child changeset directly. ## Streams + Collections Use `Phoenix.LiveView.stream/3` for any collection over ~100 items or any list that grows in response to realtime updates. `stream(socket, :messages, initial_list)` replaces `assign(socket, :messages, list)`. In the template, iterate with `
...
` — the `id` destructuring and element id are both required. Append with `stream_insert(socket, :messages, new_msg)`, remove with `stream_delete(socket, :messages, msg)`, replace with `stream_insert(socket, :messages, updated_msg)` using the same id. Streams never land in regular `@assigns`; they live under `@streams`. Common mistakes: 1. Holding a large list in `@messages` and re-rendering on every insert. 2. Forgetting the `id={id}` attribute on the iterated DOM node. 3. Calling `Enum.count/1` on `@streams.messages` — streams are opaque; track length in a separate assign. ## Mount + Lifecycle (Async Load) Use `assign_async/3` (Phoenix 1.7+) for initial data loads that touch the database. In `mount/3`, return `{:ok, assign_async(socket, :users, fn -> {:ok, %{users: Accounts.list_users()}} end)}`. The template branches on `@users.loading`, `@users.ok?`, and `@users.result` — render a skeleton while loading, render the list when `ok?`, and render an error state via `@users.failed` when the async fun returns `{:error, reason}`. In `handle_params/3`, re-run `assign_async` whenever URL params change. For multiple independent loads, call `assign_async` once per key so each resolves on its own timeline. Do not close over the whole `socket` inside the async function — capture only the primitive ids and params you need. Common mistakes: 1. Synchronously calling `Repo.all/1` directly in mount, outside the async fun — blocks first paint. 2. Forgetting to handle `@users.loading` in the template. 3. Using `assign_async` for cheap in-memory assigns where the ceremony outweighs the benefit. 4. Closing over the full `socket` inside the async fun — the closure leaks the entire assigns map. ## Event Handlers + handle_info Every `handle_event/3` clause is three lines: parse the incoming params into an `%Action{type: _, payload: _}` struct, call `handle_action(action, socket)`, and return its result directly. All state-change logic lives in `handle_action/2`, a private pure function that pattern-matches on `action.type` and returns `{:noreply, socket}`. `handle_info/2` clauses that mutate state do the same dance: convert the message into an `%Action{}` first, then delegate. Keep `handle_action/2` under ~10 clauses per component — beyond that, split the component. The payoff: `handle_action/2` is unit-testable with plain ExUnit against a built socket, with no `LiveViewTest` harness required. Common mistakes: 1. Putting `Repo.insert` or `Ecto.Changeset` logic inline in `handle_event`. 2. Letting `%Action{}` carry raw `params` maps — coerce to typed fields up front. 3. Skipping the funnel for "simple" events — inconsistency destroys the testability guarantee. 4. Forgetting to route `handle_info` state changes through `handle_action/2`. ## PubSub + Realtime The Ecto context is the single source of broadcast events. Schema writes and `Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast` live side-by-side in one function, so every caller — LiveView, Oban job, controller — fans out identically. In `MyApp.Chat`, define a private `broadcast/2` helper that receives `{:ok, result}` tuples and fans out named events. Pipe every write through it: `create_message/1`, `update_message/2`, `delete_message/1`. In the parent LiveView's `mount/3`, subscribe only when `connected?(socket)` is true: `if connected?(socket), do: Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(MyApp.PubSub, "room:#{room_id}")`. Handle incoming events in the parent and route them into children via `send_update/2` when a LiveComponent owns that slice. Never broadcast before `Repo.insert` has succeeded — always pipe the `{:ok, _}` tuple through the helper, and always include an `{:error, _}` clause to short-circuit the broadcast. Common mistakes: 1. Calling `Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast` directly from `handle_event/3` — bypasses the single source. 2. Broadcasting before `Repo.insert` returns `{:ok, _}`. 3. Making `broadcast/2` public instead of `defp` — invites ad-hoc callers. 4. Subscribing without the `connected?(socket)` guard — subscribes twice and leaks processes. ## Navigation Patterns Cross-LiveView navigation uses `push_navigate(socket, to: ~p"/...")` — mounts a fresh LiveView process. Same-LiveView URL updates (a sort change, a filter applied, a tab swap) use `push_patch(socket, to: ~p"/...")` and are handled in `handle_params/3`. Templates always use `<.link navigate={~p"/users/#{user}"}>...` for forward navigation and `<.link patch={~p"/users?sort=name"}>` for in-place patches. The legacy `live_link`, `live_patch`, and `live_redirect` helpers were removed in 1.7 and must never appear in new code. Common mistakes: 1. Using `push_redirect` (deprecated) instead of `push_navigate`. 2. Patching when the target is a different LiveView — only `push_navigate` remounts. 3. Hardcoding URL strings instead of using `~p`. ## Auth + Authz A single `on_mount` hook authenticates the user AND authorizes access to the specific resource identified by `params["id"]`. If either check fails, the hook returns `{:halt, socket |> put_flash(...) |> redirect(...)}` before the LiveView ever mounts. Define the hook in `MyAppWeb.UserAuth` — it authenticates AND fetches the resource scoped to the user via the context, e.g. `Posts.fetch_for_user/2` which returns `{:ok, post}` only when the user owns the post (or is an admin). Wire the hook per-route in the router: `live_session :post_scoped, on_mount: [{MyAppWeb.UserAuth, :scope_post}]`. Inside `mount/3`, trust `socket.assigns.post` — no re-fetch, no re-check. Pass it to LiveComponents as a prop. `handle_event/3` clauses operate on `socket.assigns.post` directly; no `if current_user.id == post.user_id` checks. Mutations still take `user` as a defense-in-depth arg at the context layer: `Posts.update_post(post, attrs, user)`. Common mistakes: 1. Re-fetching the post in `mount/3` after the hook already assigned it. 2. Putting the scoped hook on a `live_session` that mixes scoped and unscoped routes. 3. Forgetting the `params["id"]` missing case in the hook. 4. Returning `{:cont, socket}` on authz failure instead of `{:halt, ...}`. 5. Letting LiveComponents re-fetch the resource by id from their own assigns. ## Anti-Patterns Catalog Every anti-pattern below ships with a mechanical detector — grep, AST probe, or Credo check — so reviewers can find violations in any codebase by running the detector top-to-bottom. 1. **DB queries in mount/3** — `mount` runs twice (HTTP + WebSocket), duplicating every query. Detector: `rg -n 'def mount' -A 20 lib/**/*_live.ex | rg -B1 'Repo\.(all|one|get|preload)'`. Fix: wrap in `assign_async` or guard with `if connected?(socket), do: load(), else: assign(socket, :items, [])`. 2. **PubSub.subscribe without connected?** — subscribes twice on first load and leaks processes on reconnect. Detector: `rg -nP 'Phoenix\.PubSub\.subscribe' lib/ | rg -v 'connected\?'`. Credo: custom `Credo.Check.Warning.UnguardedPubSubSubscribe`. 3. **Routes.*_path / Routes.*_url leftovers** — pre-1.7 helpers; verified routes are mandatory. Detector: `rg -n 'Routes\.\w+_(path|url)' lib/ test/`. Rewrite as `~p"/users/#{id}"`. 4. **Bare %Changeset{} passed to <.form>** — skips `to_form/2`, breaks nested association handling. Detector: `rg -nP '<\.form [^>]*for=\{@(?!.*to_form)' lib/`. 5. **Enum over assigns for lists >100 items** — should be `stream/3`. Heuristic: context function name starts with `list_` and the HEEx uses `:for={item <- @items}` where `@items` is assigned via `assign`, not `stream`. 6. **assign_new for values needing fresh compute** — stale after reconnect because `assign_new` only runs when the key is missing. Detector: `rg -nP 'assign_new.*fn ->' lib/ -A 3 | rg -B1 '(Repo|utc_now)'`. 7. **Legacy navigation helpers** — `live_link`, `live_patch`, `live_redirect` removed in 1.7. Detector: `rg -nP '\b(live_link|live_patch|live_redirect)\b' lib/`. Rewrite to `<.link navigate={~p"..."}>` or `<.link patch={~p"..."}>`. 8. **LiveComponent without :id** — silently becomes stateless, loses assigns between renders. Detector: `rg -n '<\.live_component' lib/ | rg -v 'id='`. 9. **Repo calls inside function components** — function components are pure rendering; queries belong in the parent LiveView or LiveComponent. Detector: `rg -n 'def \w+\(assigns\)' lib/**/components*.ex -A 20 | rg 'Repo\.'`. 10. **PubSub.broadcast called from handle_event** — broadcasts should be context-owned so every caller fans out identically. Detector: `rg -n 'def handle_event' lib/**/*_live.ex -A 15 | rg 'PubSub\.broadcast'`. 11. **Props assigned outside update/2** — violates the LiveComponent update-discipline contract. Detector: `rg -nP 'def handle_event.*\n.*assign\(socket, :\w+, assigns\.' lib/` (multiline). 12. **Raw params map stuffed into %Action{}** — defeats the event-to-action funnel's testability. Detector: AST probe for `%Action{payload: params}` where `params` is the raw event params binding. 13. **Re-fetching a resource after on_mount scoped it** — wastes a query and opens a TOCTOU gap. Detector: `rg -n 'on_mount.*:scope_' lib/**/*_live.ex -A 30 | rg 'Posts\.get_post\|Repo\.get'`. 14. **Template <%= for %> instead of :for=** — legacy Eex inside HEEx; bypasses optimization. Detector: `rg -nP '<%=\s*for\s+' lib/**/*.html.heex lib/**/*_live.ex`.

"next" button calls push_patch(to: ~p"/users?cursor=#{next_cursor}"). Must stay in the same LiveView — do not use push_navigate.

Write lib/my_app_web/live/invoices_table_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.InvoicesTableLive). Combine filter (?status=), sort (?sort=col&dir=asc), and cursor pagination (?cursor=) via URL params read in handle_params. stream(:invoices, rows, reset: true). The fixture test_fixtures/pre_1_7_user_list.ex is a Phoenix 1.6 LiveView. Produce lib/my_app_web/live/user_list_live.ex that migrates it to Phoenix 1.7+: every route becomes ~p, every <%= live_patch/live_redirect/live_link %> becomes <.link patch/navigate=>, every <%= for %>/<%= if %> becomes :for/:if, ~L becomes ~H, push_redirect becomes push_navigate, and the LiveView loads its users in handle_params/3 (not mount/3). The fixture test_fixtures/pre_1_7_user_list.ex has both 1.6 template idioms AND queries in mount. Produce lib/my_app_web/live/user_list_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.UserListLive): (1) convert ALL route helpers to ~p, (2) convert live_patch/live_link/live_redirect to <.link>, (3) convert ~L to ~H with :for/:if, (4) move Accounts.list_users out of mount into handle_params, (5) replace push_redirect with push_navigate, (6) convert the full :for loop to use :for attribute.

Write lib/my_app_web/components/modal.ex (module MyAppWeb.Components.Modal). Define modal/1 with attrs :id (string, required), :show (boolean, default false), :on_cancel (any, default nil). Declare slot :title, slot :actions, and slot :inner_block (required). Render

{render_slot(@inner_block)}
{render_slot(@actions)}
.

Write lib/my_app_web/components/table.ex (module MyAppWeb.Components.Table). Declare table/1 with attr :rows (list, required), attr :row_id (any, default nil), attr :row_click (any, default nil), and slot :col (required, takes attr :label). Additionally, slot :action (takes attr :label). Render <table> with <thead> showing @col.label and <tbody> iterating @rows, each row rendering each :col slot and a trailing :action column. The <tr> must include phx-click binding if @row_click is set.

Write lib/my_app_web/components/filter_bar.ex (module MyAppWeb.FilterBar) as a Phoenix.LiveComponent (not a function component). It owns its own :filter assign, has a handle_event("change_filter", %{"value" => value}, socket) that updates its own state AND uses send/2 to notify the parent with {__MODULE__, {:filter_changed, value}}. It must call use MyAppWeb, :live_component. Render <form phx-change="change_filter" phx-target={@myself}>.

Write lib/my_app_web/components/filter_panel_component.ex (module MyAppWeb.FilterPanelComponent) as a Phoenix.LiveComponent. Critical: it must NOT keep a local :filter copy that drifts from the parent. update/2 always writes the incoming assigns, replacing any previous state. The component's own events are scoped to @myself (phx-target={@myself}), and on change it sends the new value to the parent via send(self(), {:filter_change, new_filter}) — the parent is the source of truth, which will re-invoke update/2 with the new assigns.

Write lib/my_app_web/live/signup_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.SignupLive). mount assigns @form = to_form(MyApp.Accounts.change_user(%MyApp.Accounts.User{})). Render a form that displays per-field error messages only if used_input?(@form[:field]) is true. Use <.input field={@form[:email]} /> etc. handle_event("validate", params, socket) re-runs the changeset with action :validate and calls to_form. Must use used_input?.

Write two files: (1) lib/my_app/checkout/form.ex (module MyApp.Checkout.Form) as an Ecto.Schema with `use Ecto.Schema`, `import Ecto.Changeset`, an embedded_schema declaring fields :cardholder, :card_last4, :zipcode, and a changeset/2 that casts + validates them. (2) lib/my_app_web/live/checkout_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.CheckoutLive) that uses MyApp.Checkout.Form (NOT the DB schema MyApp.Checkout.Order directly) for the <.form>. mount builds @form via to_form(Form.changeset(%Form{}, %{})). Decoupling the UI form from the DB schema is the key pattern.

The fixture test_fixtures/assigns_messages_inbox.ex loads ~12000 messages into assigns. Rewrite it as lib/my_app_web/live/inbox_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.InboxLive) using stream/3. The LiveView must: (1) stream(:messages, [], reset: false) in mount, (2) reset/populate the stream in handle_params via stream(..., reset: true), (3) render a container with id="messages" and phx-update="stream", iterating with {dom_id, msg}, (4) handle stream_delete for "delete" events, (5) handle stream_insert at: 0 on {:new_message, m}, (6) track a separate :message_count assign because you cannot Enum.count @streams.

Write lib/my_app_web/live/clearable_list_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.ClearableListLive). It must: (1) initialize stream(:items, initial) in mount, (2) handle_event("clear", _, socket) calls stream(socket, :items, [], reset: true) — the reset: true flag is required to clear all DOM children, (3) handle_event("add", %{"name" => name}, socket) stream_inserts a new item. Render <div id="items" phx-update="stream"> with :for iteration. The reset: true on clear is the key point.

The fixture test_fixtures/db_in_mount_dashboard.ex queries the DB in mount/3 and subscribes to PubSub without a connected?/1 guard. Produce lib/my_app_web/live/dashboard_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.DashboardLive). mount/3 must only set up the current_user + initial empty assigns. Subscribing must be gated by if connected?(socket). The queries (invoices, metrics, unread_count) must run in handle_params/3. Keep the handle_info/2 clause.

The fixture test_fixtures/db_in_mount_dashboard.ex has DB queries running twice (once per HTTP mount, once per WS mount) plus an unguarded PubSub subscribe with an insufficiently scoped topic. Produce lib/my_app_web/live/dashboard_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.DashboardLive): (1) move ALL queries (invoices, metrics, unread_count) out of mount into handle_params, (2) gate PubSub.subscribe with if connected?(socket), (3) scope the topic to the user via "dashboard:user:#{current_user.id}", (4) keep the {:new_notification, _} handle_info clause, (5) mount only assigns current_user + empty defaults. All four changes are required.

Write lib/my_app_web/live/search_form_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.SearchFormLive) that has a search form with phx-change="search" and a text input with phx-debounce="300". Also handle the case where the user hasn't submitted anything yet — only show results when used_input?(@form[:q]) is true. mount/3 builds @form via to_form(%{"q" => ""}).

Write lib/my_app_web/live/settings_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.SettingsLive). The handle_event("validate") must use a single clause with a tiny discriminating head `handle_event("validate", %{"settings" => params}, socket)` — no field destructuring in the head, no binding of assigns in the head. Field-level logic lives in the body via `Enum.reduce` over params or similar. Also write a second clause `handle_event("save", %{"settings" => params}, socket)` for submission. The fixture test_fixtures/function_head_soup.ex shows the anti-pattern.

The fixture test_fixtures/external_poll_price.ex has every connected user polling an external API once per second. Replace this pattern. No :timer.send_interval in the LiveView.

Write lib/my_app_web/live/tenant_orders_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.TenantOrdersLive) that subscribes to "orders:org:#{org_id}" (NOT "orders") gated by connected?/1. Additionally, handle_info({:new_order, order}, socket) must pattern-match against order.org_id == socket.assigns.current_org.id as a defensive check, so that even if a broadcast leaks, the UI ignores it. mount assigns :current_org from session. stream(:orders, []).

Additionally, the handle_params/3 block must properly branch on the filter param (with a default _params clause). Must not leave any deprecated call.

Also define MyApp.Scope as a struct with :user. The function must pin the user id with ^ in the query. No raw Post |> Repo.all() anywhere. Additionally, write lib/my_app_web/live/posts_live_scope.ex (module MyAppWeb.PostsLiveScope) that builds %Scope{user: current_user} and calls Posts.list_posts(scope) in handle_params.

frontmatter.description: Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView — component-forward architecture with verified routes, HEEx, streams, forms (nested cast_assoc), async mount, pubsub, on_mount authz, push_navigate. Triggers on liveview, phoenix, heex, stream, form, pubsub. NOT for Phoenix 1.6 or older.
frontmatter.name: elixir-phoenix-liveview-composite

pareto_front.skill_md_content:
--- name: elixir-phoenix-liveview-composite description: Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView — component-forward architecture with verified routes, HEEx, streams, forms (nested cast_assoc), async mount, pubsub, on_mount authz, push_navigate. Triggers on liveview, phoenix, heex, stream, form, pubsub. NOT for Phoenix 1.6 or older. --- # Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView — Composite Skill ## Quick Start The parent `Phoenix.LiveView` is a thin coordinator. Anything stateful lives in a `Phoenix.LiveComponent`. The parent owns URL state, session, and PubSub subscriptions; children own their own slice of state and talk upward via `send/2` or sideways via `send_update/2`. All rendering uses HEEx with the `~p` verified-route sigil, and all collections over ~100 items flow through `stream/3`. ## When to use - Writing a new Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView with more than one interactive region - Refactoring a bloated LiveView whose `handle_event` clauses exceed ~10 cases - Reviewing PRs where a single LiveView mutates many unrelated assigns - Converting a Phoenix 1.6 LiveView to 1.7+ idioms (verified routes, `<.link>`, streams, `assign_async`) - Introducing real-time updates, nested forms, or per-resource authorization to an existing LiveView module ## Architectural Skeleton (Foundation) Every LiveView begins as a thin parent. The parent's `mount/3` and `handle_params/3` assign only URL and session state — no feature assigns, no DB queries that render blocking payloads. For each distinct interactive region (a form, a list, a filter panel, a modal) create a `Phoenix.LiveComponent` under `lib/my_app_web/live/components/`. Give every LiveComponent a stable required `:id` assign and render it as `<.live_component module={...} id="..." ... />`. User events are handled inside the component's own `handle_event/3` and mutate only that component's socket. Child-to-parent signaling uses `send(self(), {:child_msg, payload})`, handled by the parent's `handle_info/2`; parent-to-child updates use `send_update/2` targeting a stable `id`. All database work is routed through context modules, called from the component that owns the data — never from the parent. Forms are always built with `to_form/2` inside the component that owns them, never lifted to the parent. The parent never reaches into a child's socket with `assign/3`. ## HEEx + Verified Routes Use the `~p` sigil for every route. `~p"/users/#{id}"` replaces `Routes.user_path(@socket, :show, id)`. Use HEEx directives `:if={...}` and `:for={x <- @xs}` instead of the legacy `<%= if %>` and `<%= for %>` Eex blocks. Prefer `<.link navigate={~p"/users"}>` over the removed `live_link` / `live_redirect` helpers, and `<.link patch={~p"/users?sort=name"}>` for same-LiveView URL updates. Template bodies always use the `~H` sigil. Common mistakes: 1. Mixing old `Routes.*_path` helpers with new `~p` sigils in the same module. 2. Leaving `<%= for item <- @items do %>` blocks that should be `:for={item <- @items}` directives. 3. Using `` where `<.link navigate={~p"/x"}>` is required for client-side routing. ## Function Components + Slots Function components are rendering primitives, not logic holders. Declare every input with `attr :name, :type, required: true` — no untyped assigns. Declare every child region with `slot :inner_block` or a named `slot :actions`. The body is HEEx + `render_slot/1` + `~H` interpolation only. No `case`, no branching on business rules, no Ecto calls, no `Repo.get/2`. If a rendering decision is needed — a badge color for a status, a class for an alert variant — compute it in the parent LiveView via `assign/3` and pass the resolved value in as an attr. Co-locate these components under `MyAppWeb.CoreComponents` or a feature-scoped `*Components` module. Unit-test them with `Phoenix.LiveViewTest.render_component/2` passing literal attrs. Common mistakes: 1. Calling `Repo.get/2` or context functions inside a function component. 2. Using `case @status do ... end` for class selection — precompute in the parent. 3. Accepting a raw `%Ecto.Changeset{}` and branching on `changeset.valid?`. 4. Adding `@socket` or `assign/3` calls — function components have no socket. 5. Omitting `attr` declarations "just for this one prop". ## LiveComponents (Stateful) Treat `update/2` as the single choke point where parent-supplied props become socket assigns. It is the ONLY function that calls `assign(socket, :prop, assigns.prop)` for external data. Any dependent local state (filtered lists, derived labels) is also computed inside `update/2`, in the same pass. `handle_event/3` mutates only component-owned UI state like `:open?` or `:selected_id`; it must never re-read props from `assigns`. When the parent needs to push a mid-lifecycle change, it calls `send_update(MyComponent, id: "x", prop: new_value)`. For child-to-parent signaling, use `send(self(), {:child_event, @myself, payload})` and route it through the parent's `handle_info/2`. The parent's `<.live_component />` call MUST include `:id`. Common mistakes: 1. Calling `assign(socket, :prop, assigns.prop)` inside `handle_event/3`. Props belong in `update/2` only. 2. Forgetting `:id` on `<.live_component />`. Stateful components REQUIRE a stable id. 3. Deriving filtered or sorted data in the template instead of in `update/2`. 4. Using `send_update/3` with partial assigns and expecting old props to persist. 5. Trying to mutate parent state by reaching "up" through assigns. ## Forms + Nested Associations Nested forms use `cast_assoc` plus `<.inputs_for>`. In the schema, declare `has_many :children, Child, on_replace: :delete`. In the changeset, call `cast_assoc(:children, with: &Child.changeset/2, sort_param: :children_sort, drop_param: :children_drop)`. In the LiveComponent that owns the parent form, assign the form once with `to_form(Parents.change_parent(parent))`. Render children with `<.inputs_for :let={cf} field={@form[:children]}>`. Add a child with a button posting `parent[children_sort][]=new`; remove a child with `parent[children_drop][]=` plus a hidden input inside the row. On the `"validate"` event, rebuild via `to_form(changeset, action: :validate)`. On `"save"`, call the context's `update_parent/2`; on `{:error, changeset}`, re-assign `to_form(changeset)`. Common mistakes: 1. Forgetting `on_replace: :delete` on the `has_many` — removed rows reappear after save. 2. Using `<.inputs_for>` without a matching hidden `sort_param` input — row order scrambles. 3. Rebuilding child rows manually in `handle_event` instead of letting `cast_assoc` + `:drop_param` do it. 4. Putting the nested form in the parent LiveView instead of the owning LiveComponent. 5. Calling `to_form/2` on the child changeset directly. ## Streams + Collections Use `Phoenix.LiveView.stream/3` for any collection over ~100 items or any list that grows in response to realtime updates. `stream(socket, :messages, initial_list)` replaces `assign(socket, :messages, list)`. In the template, iterate with `
...
` — the `id` destructuring and element id are both required. Append with `stream_insert(socket, :messages, new_msg)`, remove with `stream_delete(socket, :messages, msg)`, replace with `stream_insert(socket, :messages, updated_msg)` using the same id. Streams never land in regular `@assigns`; they live under `@streams`. Common mistakes: 1. Holding a large list in `@messages` and re-rendering on every insert. 2. Forgetting the `id={id}` attribute on the iterated DOM node. 3. Calling `Enum.count/1` on `@streams.messages` — streams are opaque; track length in a separate assign. ## Mount + Lifecycle (Async Load) Use `assign_async/3` (Phoenix 1.7+) for initial data loads that touch the database. In `mount/3`, return `{:ok, assign_async(socket, :users, fn -> {:ok, %{users: Accounts.list_users()}} end)}`. The template branches on `@users.loading`, `@users.ok?`, and `@users.result` — render a skeleton while loading, render the list when `ok?`, and render an error state via `@users.failed` when the async fun returns `{:error, reason}`. In `handle_params/3`, re-run `assign_async` whenever URL params change. For multiple independent loads, call `assign_async` once per key so each resolves on its own timeline. Do not close over the whole `socket` inside the async function — capture only the primitive ids and params you need. Common mistakes: 1. Synchronously calling `Repo.all/1` directly in mount, outside the async fun — blocks first paint. 2. Forgetting to handle `@users.loading` in the template. 3. Using `assign_async` for cheap in-memory assigns where the ceremony outweighs the benefit. 4. Closing over the full `socket` inside the async fun — the closure leaks the entire assigns map. ## Event Handlers + handle_info Every `handle_event/3` clause is three lines: parse the incoming params into an `%Action{type: _, payload: _}` struct, call `handle_action(action, socket)`, and return its result directly. All state-change logic lives in `handle_action/2`, a private pure function that pattern-matches on `action.type` and returns `{:noreply, socket}`. `handle_info/2` clauses that mutate state do the same dance: convert the message into an `%Action{}` first, then delegate. Keep `handle_action/2` under ~10 clauses per component — beyond that, split the component. The payoff: `handle_action/2` is unit-testable with plain ExUnit against a built socket, with no `LiveViewTest` harness required. Common mistakes: 1. Putting `Repo.insert` or `Ecto.Changeset` logic inline in `handle_event`. 2. Letting `%Action{}` carry raw `params` maps — coerce to typed fields up front. 3. Skipping the funnel for "simple" events — inconsistency destroys the testability guarantee. 4. Forgetting to route `handle_info` state changes through `handle_action/2`. ## PubSub + Realtime The Ecto context is the single source of broadcast events. Schema writes and `Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast` live side-by-side in one function, so every caller — LiveView, Oban job, controller — fans out identically. In `MyApp.Chat`, define a private `broadcast/2` helper that receives `{:ok, result}` tuples and fans out named events. Pipe every write through it: `create_message/1`, `update_message/2`, `delete_message/1`. In the parent LiveView's `mount/3`, subscribe only when `connected?(socket)` is true: `if connected?(socket), do: Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(MyApp.PubSub, "room:#{room_id}")`. Handle incoming events in the parent and route them into children via `send_update/2` when a LiveComponent owns that slice. Never broadcast before `Repo.insert` has succeeded — always pipe the `{:ok, _}` tuple through the helper, and always include an `{:error, _}` clause to short-circuit the broadcast. Common mistakes: 1. Calling `Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast` directly from `handle_event/3` — bypasses the single source. 2. Broadcasting before `Repo.insert` returns `{:ok, _}`. 3. Making `broadcast/2` public instead of `defp` — invites ad-hoc callers. 4. Subscribing without the `connected?(socket)` guard — subscribes twice and leaks processes. ## Navigation Patterns Cross-LiveView navigation uses `push_navigate(socket, to: ~p"/...")` — mounts a fresh LiveView process. Same-LiveView URL updates (a sort change, a filter applied, a tab swap) use `push_patch(socket, to: ~p"/...")` and are handled in `handle_params/3`. Templates always use `<.link navigate={~p"/users/#{user}"}>...` for forward navigation and `<.link patch={~p"/users?sort=name"}>` for in-place patches. The legacy `live_link`, `live_patch`, and `live_redirect` helpers were removed in 1.7 and must never appear in new code. Common mistakes: 1. Using `push_redirect` (deprecated) instead of `push_navigate`. 2. Patching when the target is a different LiveView — only `push_navigate` remounts. 3. Hardcoding URL strings instead of using `~p`. ## Auth + Authz A single `on_mount` hook authenticates the user AND authorizes access to the specific resource identified by `params["id"]`. If either check fails, the hook returns `{:halt, socket |> put_flash(...) |> redirect(...)}` before the LiveView ever mounts. Define the hook in `MyAppWeb.UserAuth` — it authenticates AND fetches the resource scoped to the user via the context, e.g. `Posts.fetch_for_user/2` which returns `{:ok, post}` only when the user owns the post (or is an admin). Wire the hook per-route in the router: `live_session :post_scoped, on_mount: [{MyAppWeb.UserAuth, :scope_post}]`. Inside `mount/3`, trust `socket.assigns.post` — no re-fetch, no re-check. Pass it to LiveComponents as a prop. `handle_event/3` clauses operate on `socket.assigns.post` directly; no `if current_user.id == post.user_id` checks. Mutations still take `user` as a defense-in-depth arg at the context layer: `Posts.update_post(post, attrs, user)`. Common mistakes: 1. Re-fetching the post in `mount/3` after the hook already assigned it. 2. Putting the scoped hook on a `live_session` that mixes scoped and unscoped routes. 3. Forgetting the `params["id"]` missing case in the hook. 4. Returning `{:cont, socket}` on authz failure instead of `{:halt, ...}`. 5. Letting LiveComponents re-fetch the resource by id from their own assigns. ## Anti-Patterns Catalog Every anti-pattern below ships with a mechanical detector — grep, AST probe, or Credo check — so reviewers can find violations in any codebase by running the detector top-to-bottom. 1. **DB queries in mount/3** — `mount` runs twice (HTTP + WebSocket), duplicating every query. Detector: `rg -n 'def mount' -A 20 lib/**/*_live.ex | rg -B1 'Repo\.(all|one|get|preload)'`. Fix: wrap in `assign_async` or guard with `if connected?(socket), do: load(), else: assign(socket, :items, [])`. 2. **PubSub.subscribe without connected?** — subscribes twice on first load and leaks processes on reconnect. Detector: `rg -nP 'Phoenix\.PubSub\.subscribe' lib/ | rg -v 'connected\?'`. Credo: custom `Credo.Check.Warning.UnguardedPubSubSubscribe`. 3. **Routes.*_path / Routes.*_url leftovers** — pre-1.7 helpers; verified routes are mandatory. Detector: `rg -n 'Routes\.\w+_(path|url)' lib/ test/`. Rewrite as `~p"/users/#{id}"`. 4. **Bare %Changeset{} passed to <.form>** — skips `to_form/2`, breaks nested association handling. Detector: `rg -nP '<\.form [^>]*for=\{@(?!.*to_form)' lib/`. 5. **Enum over assigns for lists >100 items** — should be `stream/3`. Heuristic: context function name starts with `list_` and the HEEx uses `:for={item <- @items}` where `@items` is assigned via `assign`, not `stream`. 6. **assign_new for values needing fresh compute** — stale after reconnect because `assign_new` only runs when the key is missing. Detector: `rg -nP 'assign_new.*fn ->' lib/ -A 3 | rg -B1 '(Repo|utc_now)'`. 7. **Legacy navigation helpers** — `live_link`, `live_patch`, `live_redirect` removed in 1.7. Detector: `rg -nP '\b(live_link|live_patch|live_redirect)\b' lib/`. Rewrite to `<.link navigate={~p"..."}>` or `<.link patch={~p"..."}>`. 8. **LiveComponent without :id** — silently becomes stateless, loses assigns between renders. Detector: `rg -n '<\.live_component' lib/ | rg -v 'id='`. 9. **Repo calls inside function components** — function components are pure rendering; queries belong in the parent LiveView or LiveComponent. Detector: `rg -n 'def \w+\(assigns\)' lib/**/components*.ex -A 20 | rg 'Repo\.'`. 10. **PubSub.broadcast called from handle_event** — broadcasts should be context-owned so every caller fans out identically. Detector: `rg -n 'def handle_event' lib/**/*_live.ex -A 15 | rg 'PubSub\.broadcast'`. 11. **Props assigned outside update/2** — violates the LiveComponent update-discipline contract. Detector: `rg -nP 'def handle_event.*\n.*assign\(socket, :\w+, assigns\.' lib/` (multiline). 12. **Raw params map stuffed into %Action{}** — defeats the event-to-action funnel's testability. Detector: AST probe for `%Action{payload: params}` where `params` is the raw event params binding. 13. **Re-fetching a resource after on_mount scoped it** — wastes a query and opens a TOCTOU gap. Detector: `rg -n 'on_mount.*:scope_' lib/**/*_live.ex -A 30 | rg 'Posts\.get_post\|Repo\.get'`. 14. **Template <%= for %> instead of :for=** — legacy Eex inside HEEx; bypasses optimization. Detector: `rg -nP '<%=\s*for\s+' lib/**/*.html.heex lib/**/*_live.ex`.

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
--- name: elixir-phoenix-liveview-composite description: Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView — component-forward architecture with verified routes, HEEx, streams, forms (nested cast_assoc), async mount, pubsub, on_mount authz, push_navigate. Triggers on liveview, phoenix, heex, stream, form, pubsub. NOT for Phoenix 1.6 or older. --- # Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView — Composite Skill ## Quick Start The parent `Phoenix.LiveView` is a thin coordinator. Anything stateful lives in a `Phoenix.LiveComponent`. The parent owns URL state, session, and PubSub subscriptions; children own their own slice of state and talk upward via `send/2` or sideways via `send_update/2`. All rendering uses HEEx with the `~p` verified-route sigil, and all collections over ~100 items flow through `stream/3`. ## When to use - Writing a new Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView with more than one interactive region - Refactoring a bloated LiveView whose `handle_event` clauses exceed ~10 cases - Reviewing PRs where a single LiveView mutates many unrelated assigns - Converting a Phoenix 1.6 LiveView to 1.7+ idioms (verified routes, `<.link>`, streams, `assign_async`) - Introducing real-time updates, nested forms, or per-resource authorization to an existing LiveView module ## Architectural Skeleton (Foundation) Every LiveView begins as a thin parent. The parent's `mount/3` and `handle_params/3` assign only URL and session state — no feature assigns, no DB queries that render blocking payloads. For each distinct interactive region (a form, a list, a filter panel, a modal) create a `Phoenix.LiveComponent` under `lib/my_app_web/live/components/`. Give every LiveComponent a stable required `:id` assign and render it as `<.live_component module={...} id="..." ... />`. User events are handled inside the component's own `handle_event/3` and mutate only that component's socket. Child-to-parent signaling uses `send(self(), {:child_msg, payload})`, handled by the parent's `handle_info/2`; parent-to-child updates use `send_update/2` targeting a stable `id`. All database work is routed through context modules, called from the component that owns the data — never from the parent. Forms are always built with `to_form/2` inside the component that owns them, never lifted to the parent. The parent never reaches into a child's socket with `assign/3`. ## HEEx + Verified Routes Use the `~p` sigil for every route. `~p"/users/#{id}"` replaces `Routes.user_path(@socket, :show, id)`. Use HEEx directives `:if={...}` and `:for={x <- @xs}` instead of the legacy `<%= if %>` and `<%= for %>` Eex blocks. Prefer `<.link navigate={~p"/users"}>` over the removed `live_link` / `live_redirect` helpers, and `<.link patch={~p"/users?sort=name"}>` for same-LiveView URL updates. Template bodies always use the `~H` sigil. Common mistakes: 1. Mixing old `Routes.*_path` helpers with new `~p` sigils in the same module. 2. Leaving `<%= for item <- @items do %>` blocks that should be `:for={item <- @items}` directives. 3. Using `` where `<.link navigate={~p"/x"}>` is required for client-side routing. ## Function Components + Slots Function components are rendering primitives, not logic holders. Declare every input with `attr :name, :type, required: true` — no untyped assigns. Declare every child region with `slot :inner_block` or a named `slot :actions`. The body is HEEx + `render_slot/1` + `~H` interpolation only. No `case`, no branching on business rules, no Ecto calls, no `Repo.get/2`. If a rendering decision is needed — a badge color for a status, a class for an alert variant — compute it in the parent LiveView via `assign/3` and pass the resolved value in as an attr. Co-locate these components under `MyAppWeb.CoreComponents` or a feature-scoped `*Components` module. Unit-test them with `Phoenix.LiveViewTest.render_component/2` passing literal attrs. Common mistakes: 1. Calling `Repo.get/2` or context functions inside a function component. 2. Using `case @status do ... end` for class selection — precompute in the parent. 3. Accepting a raw `%Ecto.Changeset{}` and branching on `changeset.valid?`. 4. Adding `@socket` or `assign/3` calls — function components have no socket. 5. Omitting `attr` declarations "just for this one prop". ## LiveComponents (Stateful) Treat `update/2` as the single choke point where parent-supplied props become socket assigns. It is the ONLY function that calls `assign(socket, :prop, assigns.prop)` for external data. Any dependent local state (filtered lists, derived labels) is also computed inside `update/2`, in the same pass. `handle_event/3` mutates only component-owned UI state like `:open?` or `:selected_id`; it must never re-read props from `assigns`. When the parent needs to push a mid-lifecycle change, it calls `send_update(MyComponent, id: "x", prop: new_value)`. For child-to-parent signaling, use `send(self(), {:child_event, @myself, payload})` and route it through the parent's `handle_info/2`. The parent's `<.live_component />` call MUST include `:id`. Common mistakes: 1. Calling `assign(socket, :prop, assigns.prop)` inside `handle_event/3`. Props belong in `update/2` only. 2. Forgetting `:id` on `<.live_component />`. Stateful components REQUIRE a stable id. 3. Deriving filtered or sorted data in the template instead of in `update/2`. 4. Using `send_update/3` with partial assigns and expecting old props to persist. 5. Trying to mutate parent state by reaching "up" through assigns. ## Forms + Nested Associations Nested forms use `cast_assoc` plus `<.inputs_for>`. In the schema, declare `has_many :children, Child, on_replace: :delete`. In the changeset, call `cast_assoc(:children, with: &Child.changeset/2, sort_param: :children_sort, drop_param: :children_drop)`. In the LiveComponent that owns the parent form, assign the form once with `to_form(Parents.change_parent(parent))`. Render children with `<.inputs_for :let={cf} field={@form[:children]}>`. Add a child with a button posting `parent[children_sort][]=new`; remove a child with `parent[children_drop][]=` plus a hidden input inside the row. On the `"validate"` event, rebuild via `to_form(changeset, action: :validate)`. On `"save"`, call the context's `update_parent/2`; on `{:error, changeset}`, re-assign `to_form(changeset)`. Common mistakes: 1. Forgetting `on_replace: :delete` on the `has_many` — removed rows reappear after save. 2. Using `<.inputs_for>` without a matching hidden `sort_param` input — row order scrambles. 3. Rebuilding child rows manually in `handle_event` instead of letting `cast_assoc` + `:drop_param` do it. 4. Putting the nested form in the parent LiveView instead of the owning LiveComponent. 5. Calling `to_form/2` on the child changeset directly. ## Streams + Collections Use `Phoenix.LiveView.stream/3` for any collection over ~100 items or any list that grows in response to realtime updates. `stream(socket, :messages, initial_list)` replaces `assign(socket, :messages, list)`. In the template, iterate with `
...
` — the `id` destructuring and element id are both required. Append with `stream_insert(socket, :messages, new_msg)`, remove with `stream_delete(socket, :messages, msg)`, replace with `stream_insert(socket, :messages, updated_msg)` using the same id. Streams never land in regular `@assigns`; they live under `@streams`. Common mistakes: 1. Holding a large list in `@messages` and re-rendering on every insert. 2. Forgetting the `id={id}` attribute on the iterated DOM node. 3. Calling `Enum.count/1` on `@streams.messages` — streams are opaque; track length in a separate assign. ## Mount + Lifecycle (Async Load) Use `assign_async/3` (Phoenix 1.7+) for initial data loads that touch the database. In `mount/3`, return `{:ok, assign_async(socket, :users, fn -> {:ok, %{users: Accounts.list_users()}} end)}`. The template branches on `@users.loading`, `@users.ok?`, and `@users.result` — render a skeleton while loading, render the list when `ok?`, and render an error state via `@users.failed` when the async fun returns `{:error, reason}`. In `handle_params/3`, re-run `assign_async` whenever URL params change. For multiple independent loads, call `assign_async` once per key so each resolves on its own timeline. Do not close over the whole `socket` inside the async function — capture only the primitive ids and params you need. Common mistakes: 1. Synchronously calling `Repo.all/1` directly in mount, outside the async fun — blocks first paint. 2. Forgetting to handle `@users.loading` in the template. 3. Using `assign_async` for cheap in-memory assigns where the ceremony outweighs the benefit. 4. Closing over the full `socket` inside the async fun — the closure leaks the entire assigns map. ## Event Handlers + handle_info Every `handle_event/3` clause is three lines: parse the incoming params into an `%Action{type: _, payload: _}` struct, call `handle_action(action, socket)`, and return its result directly. All state-change logic lives in `handle_action/2`, a private pure function that pattern-matches on `action.type` and returns `{:noreply, socket}`. `handle_info/2` clauses that mutate state do the same dance: convert the message into an `%Action{}` first, then delegate. Keep `handle_action/2` under ~10 clauses per component — beyond that, split the component. The payoff: `handle_action/2` is unit-testable with plain ExUnit against a built socket, with no `LiveViewTest` harness required. Common mistakes: 1. Putting `Repo.insert` or `Ecto.Changeset` logic inline in `handle_event`. 2. Letting `%Action{}` carry raw `params` maps — coerce to typed fields up front. 3. Skipping the funnel for "simple" events — inconsistency destroys the testability guarantee. 4. Forgetting to route `handle_info` state changes through `handle_action/2`. ## PubSub + Realtime The Ecto context is the single source of broadcast events. Schema writes and `Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast` live side-by-side in one function, so every caller — LiveView, Oban job, controller — fans out identically. In `MyApp.Chat`, define a private `broadcast/2` helper that receives `{:ok, result}` tuples and fans out named events. Pipe every write through it: `create_message/1`, `update_message/2`, `delete_message/1`. In the parent LiveView's `mount/3`, subscribe only when `connected?(socket)` is true: `if connected?(socket), do: Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe(MyApp.PubSub, "room:#{room_id}")`. Handle incoming events in the parent and route them into children via `send_update/2` when a LiveComponent owns that slice. Never broadcast before `Repo.insert` has succeeded — always pipe the `{:ok, _}` tuple through the helper, and always include an `{:error, _}` clause to short-circuit the broadcast. Common mistakes: 1. Calling `Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast` directly from `handle_event/3` — bypasses the single source. 2. Broadcasting before `Repo.insert` returns `{:ok, _}`. 3. Making `broadcast/2` public instead of `defp` — invites ad-hoc callers. 4. Subscribing without the `connected?(socket)` guard — subscribes twice and leaks processes. ## Navigation Patterns Cross-LiveView navigation uses `push_navigate(socket, to: ~p"/...")` — mounts a fresh LiveView process. Same-LiveView URL updates (a sort change, a filter applied, a tab swap) use `push_patch(socket, to: ~p"/...")` and are handled in `handle_params/3`. Templates always use `<.link navigate={~p"/users/#{user}"}>...` for forward navigation and `<.link patch={~p"/users?sort=name"}>` for in-place patches. The legacy `live_link`, `live_patch`, and `live_redirect` helpers were removed in 1.7 and must never appear in new code. Common mistakes: 1. Using `push_redirect` (deprecated) instead of `push_navigate`. 2. Patching when the target is a different LiveView — only `push_navigate` remounts. 3. Hardcoding URL strings instead of using `~p`. ## Auth + Authz A single `on_mount` hook authenticates the user AND authorizes access to the specific resource identified by `params["id"]`. If either check fails, the hook returns `{:halt, socket |> put_flash(...) |> redirect(...)}` before the LiveView ever mounts. Define the hook in `MyAppWeb.UserAuth` — it authenticates AND fetches the resource scoped to the user via the context, e.g. `Posts.fetch_for_user/2` which returns `{:ok, post}` only when the user owns the post (or is an admin). Wire the hook per-route in the router: `live_session :post_scoped, on_mount: [{MyAppWeb.UserAuth, :scope_post}]`. Inside `mount/3`, trust `socket.assigns.post` — no re-fetch, no re-check. Pass it to LiveComponents as a prop. `handle_event/3` clauses operate on `socket.assigns.post` directly; no `if current_user.id == post.user_id` checks. Mutations still take `user` as a defense-in-depth arg at the context layer: `Posts.update_post(post, attrs, user)`. Common mistakes: 1. Re-fetching the post in `mount/3` after the hook already assigned it. 2. Putting the scoped hook on a `live_session` that mixes scoped and unscoped routes. 3. Forgetting the `params["id"]` missing case in the hook. 4. Returning `{:cont, socket}` on authz failure instead of `{:halt, ...}`. 5. Letting LiveComponents re-fetch the resource by id from their own assigns. ## Anti-Patterns Catalog Every anti-pattern below ships with a mechanical detector — grep, AST probe, or Credo check — so reviewers can find violations in any codebase by running the detector top-to-bottom. 1. **DB queries in mount/3** — `mount` runs twice (HTTP + WebSocket), duplicating every query. Detector: `rg -n 'def mount' -A 20 lib/**/*_live.ex | rg -B1 'Repo\.(all|one|get|preload)'`. Fix: wrap in `assign_async` or guard with `if connected?(socket), do: load(), else: assign(socket, :items, [])`. 2. **PubSub.subscribe without connected?** — subscribes twice on first load and leaks processes on reconnect. Detector: `rg -nP 'Phoenix\.PubSub\.subscribe' lib/ | rg -v 'connected\?'`. Credo: custom `Credo.Check.Warning.UnguardedPubSubSubscribe`. 3. **Routes.*_path / Routes.*_url leftovers** — pre-1.7 helpers; verified routes are mandatory. Detector: `rg -n 'Routes\.\w+_(path|url)' lib/ test/`. Rewrite as `~p"/users/#{id}"`. 4. **Bare %Changeset{} passed to <.form>** — skips `to_form/2`, breaks nested association handling. Detector: `rg -nP '<\.form [^>]*for=\{@(?!.*to_form)' lib/`. 5. **Enum over assigns for lists >100 items** — should be `stream/3`. Heuristic: context function name starts with `list_` and the HEEx uses `:for={item <- @items}` where `@items` is assigned via `assign`, not `stream`. 6. **assign_new for values needing fresh compute** — stale after reconnect because `assign_new` only runs when the key is missing. Detector: `rg -nP 'assign_new.*fn ->' lib/ -A 3 | rg -B1 '(Repo|utc_now)'`. 7. **Legacy navigation helpers** — `live_link`, `live_patch`, `live_redirect` removed in 1.7. Detector: `rg -nP '\b(live_link|live_patch|live_redirect)\b' lib/`. Rewrite to `<.link navigate={~p"..."}>` or `<.link patch={~p"..."}>`. 8. **LiveComponent without :id** — silently becomes stateless, loses assigns between renders. Detector: `rg -n '<\.live_component' lib/ | rg -v 'id='`. 9. **Repo calls inside function components** — function components are pure rendering; queries belong in the parent LiveView or LiveComponent. Detector: `rg -n 'def \w+\(assigns\)' lib/**/components*.ex -A 20 | rg 'Repo\.'`. 10. **PubSub.broadcast called from handle_event** — broadcasts should be context-owned so every caller fans out identically. Detector: `rg -n 'def handle_event' lib/**/*_live.ex -A 15 | rg 'PubSub\.broadcast'`. 11. **Props assigned outside update/2** — violates the LiveComponent update-discipline contract. Detector: `rg -nP 'def handle_event.*\n.*assign\(socket, :\w+, assigns\.' lib/` (multiline). 12. **Raw params map stuffed into %Action{}** — defeats the event-to-action funnel's testability. Detector: AST probe for `%Action{payload: params}` where `params` is the raw event params binding. 13. **Re-fetching a resource after on_mount scoped it** — wastes a query and opens a TOCTOU gap. Detector: `rg -n 'on_mount.*:scope_' lib/**/*_live.ex -A 30 | rg 'Posts\.get_post\|Repo\.get'`. 14. **Template <%= for %> instead of :for=** — legacy Eex inside HEEx; bypasses optimization. Detector: `rg -nP '<%=\s*for\s+' lib/**/*.html.heex lib/**/*_live.ex`.

description: Explicit list of Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView anti-patterns to avoid.
---

# Iron-Law Catalog

## Iron laws
1. **NO DATABASE QUERIES IN MOUNT** — mount runs twice
2. **Always connected?(socket) before PubSub.subscribe**
3. **Always ~p, never Routes.*_path**
4. **Always to_form for forms, never bare changesets**
5. **Always stream/3 for lists >100 items**
6. **Never use assign_new for values that need fresh computation on reconnect**
7. **Never use live_link, live_patch, live_redirect — use <.link> and push_navigate/push_patch**

# Detector-Rule Catalog

## Quick Start
Every anti-pattern below ships with a detector: a grep, AST probe, or Credo check you can run against a codebase. Run the detectors top-to-bottom; each hit is a review finding.

## Anti-patterns and detectors

1. **DB queries in mount/3** — mount runs twice; duplicate queries on every page load.
   - Grep: `rg -n 'def mount' -A 20 lib/**/*_live.ex | rg -B1 'Repo\.(all|one|get|preload)'`
   - Fix: wrap in `if connected?(socket), do: load(), else: assign(socket, :items, [])`.

2. **PubSub.subscribe without connected?** — subscribes twice, leaks processes.
   - Grep: `rg -nP 'Phoenix\.PubSub\.subscribe' lib/ | rg -v 'connected\?'`
   - Credo: custom `Credo.Check.Warning.UnguardedPubSubSubscribe`.

3. **Routes.*_path / Routes.*_url leftovers** — pre-1.7 helpers; verified routes are mandatory.
   - Grep: `rg -n 'Routes\.\w+_(path|url)' lib/ test/`
   - Rewrite: `Routes.user_path(@socket, :show, id)` -> `~p"/users/#{id}"`.

4. **Bare %Changeset{} passed to <.form>** — skips `to_form/2`.
   - Grep: `rg -nP '<\.form [^>]*for=\{@(?!.*to_form)' lib/`

5. **Enum over assigns for lists > 100 items** — should be `stream/3`.
   - Heuristic: context function name starts with `list_` and HEEx uses `:for={item <- @items}`.

6. **assign_new for values needing fresh compute** — stale after reconnect.
   - Grep: `rg -nP 'assign_new.*fn ->' lib/ -A 3 | rg -B1 '(Repo|utc_now)'`

7. **Legacy navigation helpers** — `live_link`, `live_patch`, `live_redirect` removed in 1.7.
   - Grep: `rg -nP '\b(live_link|live_patch|live_redirect)\b' lib/`

name: architectural-stance--strict-liveview
description: Enforces a strict LiveView architecture: LiveView modules own state, function components are stateless, context modules handle DB. NO DB queries in mount. Use when writing or refactoring Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView code. NOT for Phoenix 1.6 or older.
---

# Strict LiveView

## Quick Start
LiveView owns state. Function components render. Context handles DB. No DB in mount.

## When to use
- Writing a new LiveView module
- Refactoring a 1.6 LiveView to 1.7
- Reviewing PRs for Phoenix architecture

## Workflow
1. Use `Phoenix.LiveView` for the top-level module
2. Move all DB queries into a context module, called from `handle_params` or `handle_event`
3. Guard side-effectful work with `if connected?(socket), do: ...`
4. Use `to_form/2` for forms, never raw changesets

## Common mistakes
- `Repo.all` in mount (runs twice: disconnected + connected)
- PubSub.subscribe without `connected?` guard
- Using `assign_new` for things that must be freshly computed

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: architectural-stance--component-forward
description: Component-forward Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView: push stateful UI into LiveComponents, keep the parent LiveView a thin coordinator that routes messages and owns URL state. Use for new LiveView modules, refactors, or PR review. NOT for Phoenix 1.6.
---

# Component-Forward LiveView

## Quick Start
Parent LiveView is a thin coordinator. Anything stateful lives in a `Phoenix.LiveComponent`. Parent owns URL + session, children own their own slice of state and talk up via `send/2` or `send_update/2`.

## When to use
- Writing a new Phoenix 1.7+ LiveView with more than one interactive region
- Refactoring a bloated LiveView whose `handle_event` clauses exceed ~10 cases
- Reviewing PRs where a single LiveView mutates many unrelated assigns

## Workflow
1. Start the parent `Phoenix.LiveView` with only URL/session state in `mount/3` and `handle_params/3`. No feature assigns here.
2. For each distinct interactive region (form, list, filter, modal), create a `Phoenix.LiveComponent` module under `lib/my_app_web/live/components/`.
3. Give every LiveComponent a required `:id` assign. Render children via `<.live_component module={...} id="..." ... />` — never inline stateful logic in the parent template.
4. Handle user events inside the LiveComponent's own `handle_event/3`. Mutate only that component's socket.
5. When a child must notify siblings or the parent, use `send(self(), {:child_msg, payload})` and handle it in the parent's `handle_info/2`. For targeted child updates, use `send_update/2` with the target component's `id`.
6. Keep all DB work in context modules. Call contexts from the LiveComponent that owns the data, not from the parent.
7. Use `to_form/2` inside the component that owns the form; never lift changesets to the parent.

## Common mistakes
- Hoisting a child's assigns into the parent "just in case" — defeats isolation and forces parent re-renders on every child change
- Omitting `:id` on a LiveComponent (it silently becomes stateless and loses its own assigns between renders)
- Calling `send_update/2` without specifying the component module, or targeting by index instead of a stable `id`
- Mutating child state from the parent via `assign/3` on the parent socket — parents cannot reach into children; use `send_update/2`
- Putting `Repo` calls directly in `handle_event/3` of either parent or component instead of routing through a context function

# on_mount Hook + Per-Event Authz

## Workflow
1. Define `on_mount :current_user` hook
2. Use `live_session :authenticated, on_mount: [{MyAppWeb.UserAuth, :ensure_authenticated}]` in router
3. In handle_event, check ownership: `if socket.assigns.current_user.id == post.user_id, do: ..., else: {:noreply, put_flash(socket, :error, "unauthorized")}`

frontmatter.description: LiveView authentication AND per-resource authorization in a single on_mount hook — halt+redirect before mount finishes, so handle_event bodies never repeat authorization checks.
frontmatter.name: auth-and-authz--resource-scope-mount

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: auth-and-authz--resource-scope-mount
description: LiveView authentication AND per-resource authorization in a single on_mount hook — halt+redirect before mount finishes, so handle_event bodies never repeat authorization checks.
---

# Resource-Scoped on_mount Authz

## Quick Start
A single `on_mount` hook authenticates the user AND authorizes access to the specific resource identified by `params["id"]`. If either fails, `{:halt, socket |> put_flash(...) |> redirect(...)}` runs before the LiveView ever mounts. `handle_event` bodies contain zero auth logic.

## Workflow
1. Define a two-purpose hook in `MyAppWeb.UserAuth` that authenticates AND fetches the resource scoped to the user.
2. Context owns the authz decision — `Posts.fetch_for_user/2` returns `{:ok, post}` only when the user owns it (or is admin).
3. Wire the hook per-route in the router via `live_session :post_scoped, on_mount: [{MyAppWeb.UserAuth, :scope_post}]`.
4. `mount/3` trusts `socket.assigns.post` — no re-fetch, no re-check. Pass it down to LiveComponents as a prop.
5. `handle_event/3` clauses operate on `socket.assigns.post` directly. No `if current_user.id == post.user_id` checks.
6. For mutations, the context still takes `user` as a defense-in-depth arg (`Posts.update_post(post, attrs, user)`).

## Common mistakes
- Re-fetching the post in `mount/3` after the hook already assigned it.
- Putting the hook on a `live_session` that mixes scoped and unscoped routes.
- Forgetting to handle the `params["id"]` missing case.
- Returning `{:cont, socket}` on authz failure instead of `{:halt, ...}`.
- Letting LiveComponents re-fetch the resource by id from their own assigns.

# Clear Separation Of Concerns

## Workflow
1. `handle_event/3` for phx-* bindings (click, submit, change, focus)
2. `handle_info/2` for PubSub broadcasts and GenServer messages
3. Never mix — don't send yourself a phx-event

frontmatter.description: Funnel handle_event clauses into tiny dispatchers that call a private handle_action/2 with a typed intent struct. Makes the event-to-state-change path unit-testable without a LiveView harness.
frontmatter.name: event-handlers-and-handle-info--event-to-action-funnel

skill_genomes.skill_md_content:
---
name: event-handlers-and-handle-info--event-to-action-funnel
description: Funnel handle_event clauses into tiny dispatchers that call a private handle_action/2 with a typed intent struct. Makes the event-to-state-change path unit-testable without a LiveView harness.
---

# Event-to-Action Funnel

## Quick Start
Every `handle_event/3` clause is three lines: parse params into an `%Action{}` struct, call `handle_action(action, socket)`, return the result. All state-change logic lives in `handle_action/2`, a pure function you can test with plain ExUnit.

## Workflow
1. Define an `%Action{}` struct at the top of the module: `defstruct [:type, :payload]`.
2. Write one `handle_event/3` clause per `phx-*` binding. Its only job: coerce params into an `%Action{}`.
3. Call `handle_action(action, socket)` and return its result directly. No business logic in `handle_event`.
4. Implement `handle_action/2` as a private function that pattern-matches on `action.type` and returns `{:noreply, socket}`.
5. `handle_info/2` clauses that mutate state also go through `handle_action/2` — convert the message into an `%Action{}` first.
6. Unit-test `handle_action/2` directly with a built socket; no LiveViewTest needed for the core logic.
7. Keep `handle_action/2` under 10 clauses per component — beyond that, split.

## Common mistakes
- Putting `Repo.insert` or `Ecto.Changeset` logic inside `handle_event`.
- Letting `%Action{}` carry raw `params` maps — coerce to typed fields.
- Skipping the funnel for "simple" events — inconsistency destroys testability.
- Forgetting to route `handle_info` state changes through `handle_action/2`.
